fault_hypothesis: On line 70, the average is computed by dividing `total` by `window` instead of `len(tail)`. When there are fewer values than the window size, this produces a smaller-than-correct average.

constraints.intent: The average should equal total divided by the actual number of tail elements, not by window.
After computing avg, verify it equals total/len(tail), catching the division-by-window bug.

spec.expr: len(tail__1) >= window or abs(avg__1 - total__1 / len(tail__1)) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: Before avg is returned, when tail is shorter than window, avg must equal total/len(tail) not total/window.

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or abs(__cbfl_result - sum(values[-window:]) / len(values[-window:])) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: The returned average must equal the mean of the actual tail elements, not total/window.
